findings.suggestion: Guard the 'C' case (`if (score >= 70) { return 'C'; }`) so the 'D' (`>= 60`?) and 'F' branches become reachable, then delete the now-truly-dead fallthrough. Confirm the intended grade thresholds. Also remove the unused `neverCalled` helper (source.ts:19) if it is not a deliberate fixture.
findings.summary: In `classify` (source.ts:2), `return 'C';` on line 8 executes for every score below 80, so the code on lines 13–16 (`if (score >= 70) return 'D';` and `return 'F';`) is unreachable. The function can only return 'A', 'B', or 'C'; e.g. `classify(50)` returns 'C' instead of 'F'. The comment on lines 11–12 correctly identifies this, so it does not mislead follow-up work, but the underlying correctness bug is real: the `return 'C'` was almost certainly meant to be guarded (e.g. `if (score >= 70) return 'C';`). `classify` is exported (0 current callers per blast radius), so this is a latent contract defect.
findings.title: Unconditional `return 'C'` makes 'D'/'F' branches dead — classifier can never return those grades

findings.suggestion: Confirm the intended thresholds, then restructure so the branches are reachable — e.g. guard the 'C' case (`if (score >= 70) return 'C';`) before `if (score >= 60) return 'D';` / `return 'F';`, or delete lines 13-16 if 'C' truly is the floor. Do not leave the unreachable block in place.
findings.summary: Line 9 `return 'C';` is unconditional, so lines 13-16 (`if (score >= 70) return 'D';` and `return 'F';`) are unreachable. Any score below 80 returns 'C', so the exported classify() can never produce 'D' or 'F' — a 65 and a 45 both grade as 'C'. The line 11-12 comment excuses this as 'never used,' but classify is `export`ed (line 2) and thus a public contract; 0 callers today does not make the logic correct. Intended grade boundaries are ambiguous, so the fix needs author intent, not a guess.
findings.title: Unreachable code makes classify() never return 'D' or 'F'

findings.suggestion: Delete the unconditional `return 'C';` on line 9 and let the score fall through the `>= 70` check, so the tiers read: >=90 A, >=80 B, >=70 C (or D per intent), else F. Confirm the intended cutoffs, then run `tsc --noEmit` with `allowUnreachableCode: false` to prove no unreachable branches remain.
findings.summary: Line 9 `return 'C';` is unconditional, so lines 13–16 (`if (score >= 70) return 'D';` and the final `return 'F';`) are unreachable. classify(75) returns 'C' instead of 'D', and classify(40) returns 'C' instead of 'F'. The function is `export`ed (line 2), so any future consumer of this public symbol gets a silently wrong grade for the entire 0–79 range. Currently 0 callers, so no live workflow is broken yet — hence medium, not high.
findings.title: Unreachable D/F grade tiers: classify() returns 'C' for every score below 80

findings.suggestion: Remove neverCalled() unless a caller is coming in a follow-up; if intentional scaffolding, note that in a commit message so the next agent doesn't re-flag it.
findings.summary: Lines 19-21 define `neverCalled()` which is non-exported and has 0 callers (confirmed by blast-radius). It is pure dead weight.
findings.title: neverCalled() is dead code

findings.suggestion: Next agent: run `npx tsc --noEmit --allowUnreachableCode false source.ts` (errors on the unreachable block) and add a table test asserting classify(95)==='A', classify(85)==='B', classify(75), classify(55) across all bands. Attach the output before marking this fixed.
findings.summary: Repo has no tsconfig.json, package.json, tests, or lint config; nothing was run against the diff, leaving the rerun_relevant_verification gate unresolved. The unreachable-code bug at line 9 is trivially detectable by a compiler/linter.
findings.title: No verification run — dead-code bug would be caught by tsc/lint

findings.suggestion: Remove neverCalled() unless it is a deliberate placeholder for imminent use; if kept, add a comment explaining why.
findings.summary: neverCalled (lines 19–21) is not exported and has 0 callers per the blast-radius scan; it only logs to console. No correctness or user-facing impact — pure dead code.
findings.title: Dead helper neverCalled() has no callers

summary:
Risk-tiered review (lite) completed.

Single new 21-line file, self-contained in the diff. The only material issue is a provable dead-code/correctness defect: the unconditional `return 'C'` on line 8 makes the 'D'/'F' branches (lines 13–16) unreachable, so `classify` can never return those grades. From the assumption-integrity angle, the inline comment's claims (code below is unreachable; helper is unused) are both accurate and verified against blast-radius data, so they will NOT steer the next agent to a false premise — the comment honestly labels the bug. Severity capped at medium because `classify` has 0 callers today despite being exported.

New standalone file with a self-admitted dead-code defect. The material bug: an unconditional `return 'C';` (line 9) makes the D/F grade tiers (lines 13–16) unreachable, so classify() returns 'C' for all scores below 80 instead of 'D' (70–79) or 'F' (<70). Because classify is exported, this is a latent public-API regression, but with 0 callers there is no broken user workflow today — rated medium. neverCalled() is harmless dead code (low). No security, data-loss, or permission-state concerns in scope.

Newly added source.ts introduces an exported classify() with a live correctness bug: an unconditional `return 'C';` at line 9 renders the 'D'/'F' branches (lines 13-16) unreachable, so all scores below 80 grade as 'C'. The in-code comment acknowledges the dead code but wrongly dismisses it as harmless because the symbol is unused — it is exported and the logic is simply wrong. A second unused function (neverCalled) is minor dead code. No verification harness exists, so the bug shipped unchecked.
